dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal uses pivot points with 5-day lookback on either side. The 'pivot' indicator is available in the allowed indicators list and represents the standard pivot point calculation. Entry is triggered when price crosses above (long) or below (short) the pivot level. Exit is the reversal condition (crosses below for long, crosses above for short). The 5-day lookback period is implicit in standard pivot point calculations.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal uses pivot points with 5-day lookback on either side. The 'pivot' indicator is available in the allowed indicators list and represents the standard pivot point calculation. Entry is triggered when price crosses above (long) or below (short) the pivot level. Exit is the reversal condition (crosses below for long, crosses above for short). The 5-day lookback period is implicit in standard pivot point calculations.

source_rule_text: Buy when price penetrates above the previous pivot point; sell when price penetrates below the previous pivot point, where pivot points are identified based on five days on either side of the pivot point.
